title: perityle villosa
intro: Perityle villosa, also known as the hairy globemallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub-like perennial plant with hairy leaves and stems, and produces small, yellow flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and is used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Perityle villosa include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
